commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can gate the lenient fallback to the intended validation error class and add focused regression coverage without choosing a new product direction.; Needs attention: The diff changes validation for untrusted MCP tool schemas and currently broadens acceptance beyond the stated typeless-inputSchema case.; Review finding: [P1] Restrict the lenient fallback to typeless input schemas — src... (sha=84ab83d7487e73bd004cdaf6a33c40635f79e0c0)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes bundled MCP tool discovery to fall back to a lenient tools/list parser for typeless root inputSchema values, normalize accepted schemas, and skip explicit non-object roots.

PR surface: Source +110, Tests +124. Total +234 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main still lets a `client.listTools()` validation error escape the bundled MCP server catalog path when the SDK rejects the `tools/list` payload. The contributor also supplied before/after live stdio MCP output, but I did not run a local repro because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can gate the lenient fallback to the intended validation error class and add focused regression coverage without choosing a new product direction.

target.title: fix: accept valid MCP tool schemas without strict inputSchema.type check
target.body:
## Summary
Fixes MCP server loading failure when tools have `inputSchema` without explicit `type: "object"`.

Closes #63602

## Changes
- `listAllTools` tries the strict SDK `client.listTools()` first, so the SDK's `cacheToolMetadata()` side effects (output-schema validators, task-support guards consumed by `callTool()`) are preserved for spec-compliant servers.
- Only when the strict page fails Zod validation (the issue #63602 failure mode) does it re-list via `client.request()` with a lenient schema (`LenientListToolsResultSchema`) that accepts `inputSchema` as any record rather than requiring `type: z.literal("object")`.
- `normalizeInputSchema()` backfills `type: "object"` only when the field is missing, preserving any explicit type the server provides.
- `isValidRootInputSchema()` + a catalog gate (`collectValidCatalogTools`) skip tools whose root `inputSchema.type` is explicitly non-object (e.g. `"string"`, `"array"`) and emit a `logWarn`. This stops malformed schemas from reaching provider tool payloads where they could fail the entire request on OpenAI-style function-call routes. Server `toolCount` reports accepted tools only.

## Root Cause
The MCP SDK v1.29.0 `ToolSchema` validates `inputSchema.type` as `z.literal("object")`. Some MCP servers (especially older SDKs or custom implementations) omit `type` from `inputSchema`, returning `{ properties: { ... } }`, which trips a Zod validation error: `invalid_value expected "object" in inputSchema.type` and crashes the whole catalog.

## Rebase notes (current revision)
- Re-ported onto current `main` after the runtime internalization refactor (#85341) renamed `pi-bundle-mcp-runtime.ts` → `agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.ts`; the fix now lives in the renamed files and follows current idiom (`sanitizeMcpMetadataText`, per-server `exposedTools` selection filter, catalog list timeout threading).
- Addressed the earlier Codex P1 about `listTools()` metadata side effects properly this time: SDK 1.29.0's `listTools()` does call `cacheToolMetadata()` (validators for `outputSchema`, required-task guards), so the lenient request is now a targeted fallback instead of a wholesale replacement. Compliant servers keep identical SDK behavior; only servers that previously crashed the catalog take the lenient path.
- Dropped the CHANGELOG edit per repo policy (release generation owns CHANGELOG.md).

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: Bundle MCP tool cataloging accepts tools whose root `inputSchema` omits `type`, backfills `type: "object"`, skips tools with explicit non-object root schemas, and reports each server's `toolCount` from accepted catalog tools only, without losing the SDK's strict-path metadata caching for compliant servers.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS from the rebased PR head, Node 25, pnpm 10, running the bundle MCP runtime suite from this branch against in-process MCP servers exercising the real `Client` listing path.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `pnpm test src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts`, `pnpm tsgo:core`, `pnpm tsgo:core:test`, `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.ts src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts`, and `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1` on both files in the rebased worktree.

Evidence after fix:
```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  34 passed (34)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard
pnpm tsgo:core / tsgo:core:test: clean
oxlint / oxfmt --check: clean
```

Observed result after fix: A `tools/list` payload whose `inputSchema` omits `type` now catalogs cleanly with `type: "object"` backfilled; a sibling tool with an explicit `type: "array"` root schema is skipped with a logWarn instead of failing the server; `toolCount` reflects only accepted tools; compliant servers still go through strict `client.listTools()`.

What was not tested: A live third-party MCP server connected to an actual model-provider tool call was not tested in this environment.
target.author: zozo123
